Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails using search_emails with query "Mehta" (or similar) in folder_name "INBOX" to find all recent emails about the Mehta relocation. Expected findings: (a) Patricia Langford's escalation email (email_inject_langford_mehta_escalation_001) from patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io demanding a comprehensive status update by Monday morning April 27 covering housing, equipment, and prenatal care she threatens escalation to Elena Rostova; CC'd to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. (b) Greg Pallone's email (email_inject_swift_mehta_equipment_001) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com reporting Swift's hazmat-certified driver Marcus on medical leave (back injury, 6-8 weeks), Swift cannot handle equipment leg, recommends KeyMove Specialty Transport with lead driver Diane Voss at $4,800. (c) Carmen Reyes's email (email_inject_urbannest_mehta_housing_001) from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com reporting Option B (The Pinnacle, Streeterville, Unit 3415) pulled tenant renewed lease; Options A (Langham Residences, River North, $1,250/wk) and C (Elysian, Gold Coast, $1,450/wk) still available with 48-hour holds expiring Monday at noon. (d) Dr. Mehta's reply (email_inject_mehta_housing_reply_001) from anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io saying he and Priya lean toward Option A but has a parking concern listing shows "street parking only" and asks about prenatal care referral at Northwestern Prentice for Priya (Dr. Kowalski in Boston needs to know which OB practice to send records to).
Search emails using search_emails with query "Mehta" (or similar) in folder_name "SENT" to find previously sent emails and establish context about what Fatimah has already communicated to Dr. Mehta and others. Expected findings sent emails including the housing options email to Dr. Mehta with details on Options A, B, and C (including that Option A at The Langham has building garage parking at $275/month this resolves Dr. Mehta's parking concern found in OE 1d).
Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Mehta" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the Mehta relocation across all channels. Expected findings: multiple messages across #operations (C006) and other channels discussing the Mehta move, including Chloe Vance's message about the DOT audit scope affecting the equipment shipment (thread_ts 1777074300.000000 in C006), and Fatimah's separate top-level message in #operations (ts 1777152600.000000) summarizing the weekend crises.
Retrieve the Slack thread replies in #operations using conversations_replies with channel_id "C006" and thread_ts "1777074300.000000" (or similar) to find Chloe Vance's detailed DOT confirmation. Expected findings: Chloe's reply (ts 1777159800.000000) confirming the Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors contain CR2032 lithium coin cells for NVRAM calibration memory backup, that under revised PHMSA guidance (49 CFR 173.185) the shipment requires a Shipper Declaration for Dangerous Goods (IATA DGR format), Class 9 lithium battery handling label on outer packaging, and that the transport vendor must have an active DOT hazmat endorsement on their CDL. Standard chain-of-custody paperwork alone is not sufficient.
Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Canopy" or "MSA" or "overage" (or similar) filtering in channel C002 (#customer-engagement) or broadly, to find Mina Hashimoto's message about cost thresholds. Expected findings: Mina's message (ts 1777081200.000000 in C002) reporting that she spoke with Patricia Langford who is unhappy, that David confirmed any overage above $2,000 on a single line item needs Canopy sign-off per MSA section 4.2, and that Fatimah should loop Mina in before confirming any vendor so they can coordinate messaging to Patricia.
List Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel,private_channel" (or similar) to identify relevant channels including #operations (C006) and #customer-engagement (C002). Expected findings: channel list including C006 (#operations) and C002 (#customer-engagement) among others, enabling targeted channel searches and message posting.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "Mehta" (or similar) to find the existing tracking tickets. Expected findings: three issues (a) linear_issue_bd446671ceed (#343, Temporary Housing), (b) linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a (#375, Equipment Packing/Chain-of-Custody), and (c) linear_issue_4902f5c68acb (#396, Household Move). All assigned to Fatimah, all currently "In Progress."
OE 8: List Linear comments using linear_list_comments with issueId "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a" (or similar) to find comments on the equipment ticket #375. Expected findings: Chloe Vance's comment (comment_inject_chloe_dot_confirm_001) confirming DOT lithium battery requirements CR2032 cells, Section II exception under 49 CFR 173.185, requirements for Shipper Declaration for Dangerous Goods, Class 9 labeling, and DOT hazmat CDL endorsement. Also, a comment (comment_inject_swift_equipment_001) about the Swift driver situation and KeyMove recommendation.
OE 9: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_canopy"] (or similar) to find the logged engagement about Patricia Langford's escalation call. Expected findings: engagement eng_inject_mehta_patricia_call_001 titled "Mehta Relocation Status Call - Escalation Warning" from April 24, documenting Patricia's call with her three specific concerns: (1) housing confirmation still pending Dr. Mehta was leaning toward Streeterville Option B, (2) equipment chain-of-custody plan not yet confirmed with Swift, (3) no update on prenatal care transition for Priya. Patricia indicated escalation to Elena Rostova if not resolved by Monday April 27.
OE 10: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Canopy" (or similar) to find the Canopy Health company record and its company_id. Expected findings: company_canopy, Canopy Health, the client for Dr. Mehta's relocation. This company_id is needed for the crm_list_engagements call in OE 9.
OE 11: Search Airtable records using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Mehta" (or similar) to find the relocation record. Expected findings: record recReloc00000030 for Dr. Anand Mehta with stale Move Start Date "2026-05-03" and Move End Date "2026-05-04" (incorrect actual target pack date is April 30 with delivery May 2-3), and a sparse Special Requirements field missing equipment serial number, vendor change details, DOT compliance info, and cost impact.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria matching DocNumber "EST-2026-0422" (or similar) to find the original cost estimate for the Mehta relocation. Expected findings: estimate EST-2026-0422 for $12,170 issued to Canopy Health, with line items including equipment handling at $2,000 ($1,200 chain-of-custody + $800 specialty crating). The revised estimate with KeyMove would be approximately $14,970 ($12,170 + $2,800 overage).
OE 13: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria matching "Swift" and "KeyMove" (or similar) to verify vendor records. Expected findings: Swift Relocations and KeyMove Specialty Transport as registered vendors in QuickBooks.
OE 14: Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina" (or similar) to find Catalina Dubois's contact information for notification. Expected findings: Catalina Dubois, Account Manager at MoveOps, email catalina.dubois@moveops.com, she manages the Canopy Health account and needs to be notified about Patricia's dissatisfaction.
OE 15: Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Diane Voss" or "KeyMove" (or similar) to verify whether the new vendor contact already exists. Expected findings: contact_keymove_diane_voss, Diane Voss at KeyMove Specialty Transport, already in the contacts system. If not found, the agent should add her using contacts_add_new_contact.
OE 16: Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Carmen Reyes" or "Greg Pallone" (or similar) to verify vendor contacts are on file. Expected findings: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions and Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations, checking whether these contacts exist and adding them via contacts_add_new_contact if missing.
OE 17: Reply to Patricia Langford's escalation email using reply_to_email with email_id "email_inject_langford_mehta_escalation_001", sender "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", and cc including "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com" and/or "chloe.vance@moveops.com" (or similar). The content should be a comprehensive status update covering: (a) housing, Option B lost, Options A and C remaining, Dr. Mehta prefers Option A, parking concern resolved (garage at $275/month), Monday noon deadline for holds; (b) equipment, Swift driver on medical leave, KeyMove Specialty Transport replacing for equipment leg at $4,800, Diane Voss as lead driver, DOT hazmat compliance required (CR2032 lithium batteries, Class 9), KeyMove booking deadline Wednesday April 29; (c) cost impact, revised estimate from $12,170 to approximately $14,970, $2,800 overage requires Canopy sign-off per MSA section 4.2; (d) prenatal care, Dr. Kowalski in Boston ready to transfer Priya's records but needs OB practice recommendation at Northwestern Prentice; (e) revised timeline with key dates.
OE 18: Update Linear issue #343 (housing) using linear_update_issue with id "linear_issue_bd446671ceed" and updated description reflecting: Option B (Streeterville/Pinnacle) no longer available, Options A (Langham, $1,250/wk) and C (Elysian, $1,450/wk) remaining, Dr. Mehta prefers Option A but has parking concern (resolvable - garage at $275/month), holds expire Monday noon April 27, prenatal care referral pending.
OE 19: Update Linear issue #375 (equipment) using linear_update_issue with id "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a" and updated description reflecting: Swift cannot handle equipment (driver on medical leave), KeyMove Specialty Transport taking over at $4,800 (+$2,800 over original $2,000), Diane Voss as lead driver, DOT hazmat requirements (CR2032 lithium cells, Class 9, Shipper Declaration for Dangerous Goods, CDL hazmat endorsement), booking deadline Wednesday April 29 (72-hour advance required for May 2 pickup), MSA 4.2 sign-off required.
OE 20: Update Linear issue #396 (household move) using linear_update_issue with id "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb" and updated description reflecting: Swift Relocations confirmed for household portion only, equipment now handled separately by KeyMove, need to coordinate delivery timing between both vendors, delivery address pending housing confirmation Monday.
OE 21: Update Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01", record id "recReloc00000030" to correct the stale dates (Move Start Date and/or Move End Date should reflect the actual pack/delivery timeline rather than the incorrect May 3-4) and expand the Special Requirements field with equipment details (serial numbers RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, SentrySafe filing cabinet), vendor change (Swift household only, KeyMove for equipment at $4,800), DOT hazmat compliance requirements, cost impact ($12,170 to ~$14,970), and housing status.
OE 22: Post a summary message to #operations using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006" and a comprehensive payload covering all three workstreams (housing, equipment, household), the DOT compliance situation, cost impact summary, Patricia's escalation threat, critical deadlines (Monday noon housing hold, Wednesday April 29 KeyMove booking), and action items for the team. The post should tag or reference relevant team members (Chloe Vance, Mina Hashimoto).
OE 23: Send an email to Catalina Dubois using send_email with sender "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com" and recipients including "catalina.dubois@moveops.com" (or similar, agent may also try Slack DM first and fall back to email). The content should alert Catalina that Patricia Langford is unhappy about the Mehta relocation, summarize the key issues (housing loss, vendor change, cost overage, DOT compliance), and note that Patricia may reach out to Catalina directly since Catalina manages the Canopy account.
OE 24: Check Fatimah's Monday calendar using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime and end_datetime covering April 27, 2026 (or calendar_read_today_calendar_events, or calendar_search_events with query "Fatimah" or similar) to identify any scheduling conflicts for Monday morning. Expected findings: three events on April 27, (a) Weekly Ops Team Standup 9:00-9:30 AM, (b) Heartland Movers - Webb/GreenStack Status Check 10:00-10:30 AM, (c) DOT Hazmat Certification Module 4 self-study 2:00-3:00 PM. This is relevant for planning the Monday morning response timeline.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 19: Update Linear issue #375 (equipment) using linear_update_issue with id "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a" and updated description reflecting: Swift cannot handle equipment (driver on medical leave), KeyMove Specialty Transport taking over at $4,800 (+$2,800 over original $2,000), Diane Voss as lead driver, DOT hazmat requirements (CR2032 lithium cells, Class 9, Shipper Declaration for Dangerous Goods, CDL hazmat endorsement), booking deadline Wednesday April 29 (72-hour advance required for May 2 pickup), MSA 4.2 sign-off required.
OE 20: Update Linear issue #396 (household move) using linear_update_issue with id "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb" and updated description reflecting: Swift Relocations confirmed for household portion only, equipment now handled separately by KeyMove, need to coordinate delivery timing between both vendors, delivery address pending housing confirmation Monday.
OE 21: Update Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01", record id "recReloc00000030" to correct the stale dates (Move Start Date and/or Move End Date should reflect the actual pack/delivery timeline rather than the incorrect May 3-4) and expand the Special Requirements field with equipment details (serial numbers RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, SentrySafe filing cabinet), vendor change (Swift household only, KeyMove for equipment at $4,800), DOT hazmat compliance requirements, cost impact ($12,170 to ~$14,970), and housing status.
OE 22: Post a summary message to #operations using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006" and a comprehensive payload covering all three workstreams (housing, equipment, household), the DOT compliance situation, cost impact summary, Patricia's escalation threat, critical deadlines (Monday noon housing hold, Wednesday April 29 KeyMove booking), and action items for the team. The post should tag or reference relevant team members (Chloe Vance, Mina Hashimoto).
OE 23: Send an email to Catalina Dubois using send_email with sender "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com" and recipients including "catalina.dubois@moveops.com" (or similar, agent may also try Slack DM first and fall back to email). The content should alert Catalina that Patricia Langford is unhappy about the Mehta relocation, summarize the key issues (housing loss, vendor change, cost overage, DOT compliance), and note that Patricia may reach out to Catalina directly since Catalina manages the Canopy account.
OE 24: Check Fatimah's Monday calendar using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime and end_datetime covering April 27, 2026 (or calendar_read_today_calendar_events, or calendar_search_events with query "Fatimah" or similar) to identify any scheduling conflicts for Monday morning. Expected findings: three events on April 27, (a) Weekly Ops Team Standup 9:00-9:30 AM, (b) Heartland Movers - Webb/GreenStack Status Check 10:00-10:30 AM, (c) DOT Hazmat Certification Module 4 self-study 2:00-3:00 PM. This is relevant for planning the Monday morning response timeline.
